seo.title: Events & Keynotes — AI adoption workshops, hackathons, keynotes
seo.description: Sector-adapted AI workshops, internal hackathons and keynotes. 90 min – 2 days. 15 to 500 people. Every demo built around your workflows, not generic productivity examples.
seo.keywords: AI keynote, AI workshop, AI hackathon, corporate AI event, AI speaker EU, Copilot workshop

hero.bannerAlt: Speaker on stage in front of large audience
hero.title: They leave with something they built.
hero.subtitle: Workshops, hackathons, and keynotes for companies that need fast proof, internal momentum, or a way to show leadership what AI actually looks like in practice.

intro.kicker: Why most AI events don't work
intro.titleStart: They show what AI can do.
intro.titleEm: Not what it does inside your company.
intro.intro: People watch a demo. Maybe try something once. Then go back to old workflows by Tuesday morning. The reason isn't the tool, it's that nothing in the session connected to their actual work. Their reports. Their client calls. Their weekly prep. When AI doesn't map to a specific task someone does every day, it stays a curiosity.
intro.pullQuoteStart: Before every session, I research the company, the sector, and the teams in the room.
intro.pullQuoteEm: The demos are built around their workflows, not generic productivity examples.
intro.pullQuoteEnd: Everyone leaves with prompt sheets they can copy-paste on Monday morning.

formats.titleEnd: real outputs, not slides about outputs.

items.title: Keynote + Q&A
items.body: Built for conferences and large corporate events. Topics include AI literacy, EU AI Act readiness, rapid prototyping, and future of work. Content is adapted to your audience and sector, not recycled from the previous event.

items.title: Hands-on workshop
items.body: Participants work inside the AI tools they already have access to. Live demos include real failures, because that's where teams build actual trust in the tool. Every participant leaves with something they built themselves.

items.title: Internal hackathon
items.body: Full design and facilitation of an internal AI hackathon. Includes brief design, team coaching, judging criteria, and an output showcase. Teams build working prototypes on real company problems, not presentations about prototypes.

items: Pre-event research on the company, sector, and specific teams in the room
All content and demos built around your tool stack and industry context
Live facilitation by Răzvan Vâlceanu, founder of The Unlearning School
Copy-paste prompt sheets every participant takes out of the room
Post-session resource pack: prompts, workflow examples, next steps
Optional: scoped proposal for follow-up adoption work based on what surfaces in the room

pricing.body: A 30-minute call is enough to confirm the format, the audience, and the right scope. From there we send a specific proposal within 48 hours.
pricing.italic: No catalogue pricing. Every event is built around the room it is delivered in.

quote.body: Your AI program might already be funded. You just haven't structured it that way. Microsoft, AWS, and Google allocate co-sell and Market Development Funds every quarter to support exactly this kind of adoption work. Most companies qualify. Most don't know it.

cta.title: Tell me about your event.
cta.subtitle: A 30-minute call to confirm format, audience, and scope. Specific proposal within 48 hours. Built around the people in the room, not the previous event we ran.
cta.note: Free · No commitment · Proposal within 48 hours
